182	func (ctrl *Controller) addMachineConfigPool(obj interface{}) {
183		pool := obj.(*mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool)
184		klog.V(4).Infof("Adding MachineConfigPool %s", pool.Name)
185		ctrl.enqueueMachineConfigPool(pool)
186	
187	}
188	
189	func (ctrl *Controller) updateMachineConfigPool(old, cur interface{}) {
190		oldPool := old.(*mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool)
191		curPool := cur.(*mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool)
192	
193		klog.V(4).Infof("Updating MachineConfigPool %s", oldPool.Name)
194		ctrl.enqueueMachineConfigPool(curPool)
195	
196		// If the worker pool's osImageStream changed, enqueue all custom pools that might inherit from it
197		if curPool.Name == ctrlcommon.MachineConfigPoolWorker &&
198			oldPool.Spec.OSImageStream.Name != curPool.Spec.OSImageStream.Name {
199			klog.V(4).Infof("Worker pool osImageStream changed from %q to %q, enqueuing custom pools",
200				oldPool.Spec.OSImageStream.Name, curPool.Spec.OSImageStream.Name)
201			ctrl.enqueueCustomPools()
202		}
203	}
204	
205	func (ctrl *Controller) deleteMachineConfigPool(obj interface{}) {
206		pool, ok := obj.(*mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool)
207		if !ok {
208			tombstone, ok := obj.(cache.DeletedFinalStateUnknown)
209			if !ok {
210				utilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf("couldn't get object from tombstone %#v", obj))
211				return
212			}
213			pool, ok = tombstone.Obj.(*mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool)
214			if !ok {
215				utilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf("tombstone contained object that is not a MachineConfigPool %#v", obj))
216				return
217			}
218		}
219		klog.V(4).Infof("Deleting MachineConfigPool %s", pool.Name)
220		// TODO(abhinavdahiya): handle deletes.
221	}
222	
223	func (ctrl *Controller) addMachineConfig(obj interface{}) {
224		mc := obj.(*mcfgv1.MachineConfig)
225		if mc.DeletionTimestamp != nil {
226			ctrl.deleteMachineConfig(mc)
227			return
228		}
229	
230		controllerRef := metav1.GetControllerOf(mc)
231		if controllerRef != nil {
232			if pool := ctrl.resolveControllerRef(controllerRef); pool != nil {
233				klog.V(4).Infof("MachineConfig %s added", mc.Name)
234				ctrl.enqueueMachineConfigPool(pool)
235				return
236			}
237		}
238	
239		pools, err := ctrl.getPoolsForMachineConfig(mc)
240		if err != nil {
241			klog.Errorf("error finding pools for machineconfig: %v", err)
242			return
243		}
244	
245		klog.V(4).Infof("MachineConfig %s added", mc.Name)
246		for _, p := range pools {
247			ctrl.enqueueMachineConfigPool(p)
248		}
249	}
250	
251	func (ctrl *Controller) updateMachineConfig(old, cur interface{}) {
252		oldMC := old.(*mcfgv1.MachineConfig)
253		curMC := cur.(*mcfgv1.MachineConfig)
254	
255		// return if spec hasn't changed
256		if reflect.DeepEqual(oldMC.Spec, curMC.Spec) {
257			return
258		}
259		klog.V(4).Infof("MachineConfig %s updated", curMC.Name)
260	
261		curControllerRef := metav1.GetControllerOf(curMC)
262		oldControllerRef := metav1.GetControllerOf(oldMC)
263		controllerRefChanged := !reflect.DeepEqual(curControllerRef, oldControllerRef)
264		if controllerRefChanged && oldControllerRef != nil {
265			klog.Errorf("machineconfig has changed controller, not allowed.")
266			return
267		}
268	
269		if curControllerRef != nil {
270			if pool := ctrl.resolveControllerRef(curControllerRef); pool != nil {
271				ctrl.enqueueMachineConfigPool(pool)
272				return
273			}
274		}
275	
276		pools, err := ctrl.getPoolsForMachineConfig(curMC)
277		if err != nil {
278			klog.Errorf("error finding pools for machineconfig: %v", err)
279			return
280		}
281		for _, p := range pools {
282			ctrl.enqueueMachineConfigPool(p)
283		}
284	}
285	
286	func (ctrl *Controller) updateOSImageStream(old, cur interface{}) {
287		oldOSImageStream := old.(*mcfgv1.OSImageStream)
288		curOSImageStream := cur.(*mcfgv1.OSImageStream)
289	
290		// return if status hasn't changed
291		// The operator components only reacts to what the OSImageStream
292		// publish in its status subresource.
293		if reflect.DeepEqual(oldOSImageStream.Status, curOSImageStream.Status) {
294			return
295		}
296		klog.V(4).Info("OSImageStream updated")
297	
298		pList, err := ctrl.mcpLister.List(labels.Everything())
299		if err != nil {
300			klog.Errorf("error listing MCPs for OSImageStream update: %v", err)
301		}
302	
303		for _, p := range pList {
304			ctrl.enqueueMachineConfigPool(p)
305		}
306	}
307	
308	func (ctrl *Controller) deleteMachineConfig(obj interface{}) {
309		mc, ok := obj.(*mcfgv1.MachineConfig)
310	
311		if !ok {
312			tombstone, ok := obj.(cache.DeletedFinalStateUnknown)
313			if !ok {
314				utilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf("couldn't get object from tombstone %#v", obj))
315				return
316			}
317			mc, ok = tombstone.Obj.(*mcfgv1.MachineConfig)
318			if !ok {
319				utilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf("tombstone contained object that is not a MachineConfig %#v", obj))
320				return
321			}
322		}
323	
324		controllerRef := metav1.GetControllerOf(mc)
325		if controllerRef != nil {
326			if pool := ctrl.resolveControllerRef(controllerRef); pool != nil {
327				klog.V(4).Infof("MachineConfig %s deleted", mc.Name)
328				ctrl.enqueueMachineConfigPool(pool)
329				return
330			}
331		}
332	
333		pools, err := ctrl.getPoolsForMachineConfig(mc)
334		if err != nil {
335			klog.Errorf("error finding pools for machineconfig: %v", err)
336			return
337		}
338	
339		klog.V(4).Infof("MachineConfig %s deleted", mc.Name)
340		for _, p := range pools {
341			ctrl.enqueueMachineConfigPool(p)
342		}
343	}
344	
345	func (ctrl *Controller) resolveControllerRef(controllerRef *metav1.OwnerReference) *mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool {
346		// We can't look up by UID, so look up by Name and then verify UID.
347		// Don't even try to look up by Name if it's the wrong Kind.
348		if controllerRef.Kind != controllerKind.Kind {
349			return nil
350		}
351		pool, err := ctrl.mcpLister.Get(controllerRef.Name)
352		if err != nil {
353			return nil
354		}
355	
356		if pool.UID != controllerRef.UID {
357			// The controller we found with this Name is not the same one that the
358			// ControllerRef points to.
359			return nil
360		}
361		return pool
362	}
363	
364	func (ctrl *Controller) getPoolsForMachineConfig(config *mcfgv1.MachineConfig) ([]*mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool, error) {
365		if len(config.Labels) == 0 {
366			return nil, fmt.Errorf("no MachineConfigPool found for MachineConfig %v because it has no labels", config.Name)
367		}
368	
369		pList, err := ctrl.mcpLister.List(labels.Everything())
370		if err != nil {
371			return nil, err
372		}
373	
374		var pools []*mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool
375		for _, p := range pList {
376			selector, err := metav1.LabelSelectorAsSelector(p.Spec.MachineConfigSelector)
377			if err != nil {
378				return nil, fmt.Errorf("invalid label selector: %w", err)
379			}
380	
381			// If a pool with a nil or empty selector creeps in, it should match nothing, not everything.
382			if selector.Empty() || !selector.Matches(labels.Set(config.Labels)) {
383				continue
384			}
385	
386			pools = append(pools, p)
387		}
388	
389		if len(pools) == 0 {
390			return nil, fmt.Errorf("could not find any MachineConfigPool set for MachineConfig %s with labels: %v", config.Name, config.Labels)
391		}
392		return pools, nil
393	}
394	
395	func (ctrl *Controller) enqueue(pool *mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool) {
396		key, err := cache.DeletionHandlingMetaNamespaceKeyFunc(pool)
397		if err != nil {
398			utilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf("couldn't get key for object %#v: %w", pool, err))
399			return
400		}
401	
402		ctrl.queue.Add(key)
403	}
404	
405	func (ctrl *Controller) enqueueRateLimited(pool *mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool) {
406		key, err := cache.DeletionHandlingMetaNamespaceKeyFunc(pool)
407		if err != nil {
408			utilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf("couldn't get key for object %#v: %w", pool, err))
409			return
410		}
411	
412		ctrl.queue.AddRateLimited(key)
413	}
414	
415	// enqueueAfter will enqueue a pool after the provided amount of time.
416	func (ctrl *Controller) enqueueAfter(pool *mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool, after time.Duration) {
417		key, err := cache.DeletionHandlingMetaNamespaceKeyFunc(pool)
418		if err != nil {
419			utilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf("couldn't get key for object %#v: %w", pool, err))
420			return
421		}
422	
423		ctrl.queue.AddAfter(key, after)
424	}

427	func (ctrl *Controller) enqueueDefault(pool *mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool) {
428		ctrl.enqueueAfter(pool, renderDelay)
429	}

431	// enqueueCustomPools enqueues all custom pools (pools that are not master, worker, or arbiter).
432	// This is used when the worker pool changes in a way that might affect custom pools that inherit from it.
433	func (ctrl *Controller) enqueueCustomPools() {
434		pools, err := ctrl.mcpLister.List(labels.Everything())
435		if err != nil {
436			klog.Warningf("Failed to list pools when enqueuing custom pools: %v", err)
437			return
438		}
439	
440		for _, pool := range pools {
441			if ctrlcommon.IsCustomPool(pool) {
442				klog.V(4).Infof("Enqueuing custom pool %s due to worker pool change", pool.Name)
443				ctrl.enqueueMachineConfigPool(pool)
444			}
445		}
446	}
447	
448	// worker runs a worker thread that just dequeues items, processes them, and marks them done.
449	// It enforces that the syncHandler is never invoked concurrently with the same key.
450	func (ctrl *Controller) worker() {
451		for ctrl.processNextWorkItem() {
452		}
453	}
454	
455	func (ctrl *Controller) processNextWorkItem() bool {
456		key, quit := ctrl.queue.Get()
457		if quit {
458			return false
459		}
460		defer ctrl.queue.Done(key)
461	
462		err := ctrl.syncHandler(key)
463		ctrl.handleErr(err, key)
464	
465		return true
466	}
467	
468	func (ctrl *Controller) handleErr(err error, key string) {
469		if err == nil {
470			ctrl.queue.Forget(key)
471			return
472		}
473	
474		if ctrl.queue.NumRequeues(key) < maxRetries {
475			klog.V(2).Infof("Error syncing machineconfigpool %v: %v", key, err)
476			ctrl.queue.AddRateLimited(key)
477			return
478		}
479	
480		utilruntime.HandleError(err)
481		klog.V(2).Infof("Dropping machineconfigpool %q out of the queue: %v", key, err)
482		ctrl.queue.Forget(key)
483		ctrl.queue.AddAfter(key, 1*time.Minute)
484	}
485	
486	// syncMachineConfigPool will sync the machineconfig pool with the given key.
487	// This function is not meant to be invoked concurrently with the same key.
488	func (ctrl *Controller) syncMachineConfigPool(key string) error {
489		startTime := time.Now()
490		klog.V(4).Infof("Started syncing machineconfigpool %q (%v)", key, startTime)
491		defer func() {
492			klog.V(4).Infof("Finished syncing machineconfigpool %q (%v)", key, time.Since(startTime))
493		}()
494	
495		_, name, err := cache.SplitMetaNamespaceKey(key)
496		if err != nil {
497			return err
498		}
499		machineconfigpool, err := ctrl.mcpLister.Get(name)
500		if errors.IsNotFound(err) {
501			klog.V(2).Infof("MachineConfigPool %v has been deleted", key)
502			return nil
503		}
504		if err != nil {
505			return err
506		}
507	
508		// Deep-copy otherwise we are mutating our cache.
509		// TODO: Deep-copy only when needed.
510		pool := machineconfigpool.DeepCopy()
511		everything := metav1.LabelSelector{}
512		if reflect.DeepEqual(pool.Spec.MachineConfigSelector, &everything) {
513			ctrl.eventRecorder.Eventf(pool, corev1.EventTypeWarning, "SelectingAll", "This machineconfigpool is selecting all machineconfigs. A non-empty selector is required.")
514			return nil
515		}
516	
517		selector, err := metav1.LabelSelectorAsSelector(pool.Spec.MachineConfigSelector)
518		if err != nil {
519			return err
520		}
521	
522		// TODO(runcom): add tests in render_controller_test.go for this condition
523		if err := apihelpers.IsControllerConfigCompleted(ctrlcommon.ControllerConfigName, ctrl.ccLister.Get); err != nil {
524			return err
525		}
526	
527		if err := apihelpers.AreMCGeneratingSubControllersCompletedForPool(ctrl.crcLister.List, ctrl.mckLister.List, pool.Labels); err != nil {
528			return err
529		}
530	
531		mcs, err := ctrl.mcLister.List(selector)
532		if err != nil {
533			return err
534		}
535		sort.SliceStable(mcs, func(i, j int) bool { return mcs[i].Name < mcs[j].Name })
536	
537		if len(mcs) == 0 {
538			return ctrl.syncFailingStatus(pool, fmt.Errorf("no MachineConfigs found matching selector %v", selector))
539		}
540	
541		if err := ctrl.syncGeneratedMachineConfig(pool, mcs); err != nil {
542			klog.Errorf("Error syncing Generated MCFG: %v", err)
543			return ctrl.syncFailingStatus(pool, err)
544		}
545		return ctrl.syncAvailableStatus(pool)
546	}
547	
548	func (ctrl *Controller) syncAvailableStatus(pool *mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool) error {
549		if apihelpers.IsMachineConfigPoolConditionFalse(pool.Status.Conditions, mcfgv1.MachineConfigPoolRenderDegraded) {
550			return nil
551		}
552		sdegraded := apihelpers.NewMachineConfigPoolCondition(mcfgv1.MachineConfigPoolRenderDegraded, corev1.ConditionFalse, "", "")
553		apihelpers.SetMachineConfigPoolCondition(&pool.Status, *sdegraded)
554		if _, err := ctrl.client.MachineconfigurationV1().MachineConfigPools().UpdateStatus(context.TODO(), pool, metav1.UpdateOptions{}); err != nil {
555			return err
556		}
557		return nil
558	}
559	
560	func (ctrl *Controller) syncFailingStatus(pool *mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool, err error) error {
561		sdegraded := apihelpers.NewMachineConfigPoolCondition(mcfgv1.MachineConfigPoolRenderDegraded, corev1.ConditionTrue, "", fmt.Sprintf("Failed to render configuration for pool %s: %v", pool.Name, err))
562		apihelpers.SetMachineConfigPoolCondition(&pool.Status, *sdegraded)
563		if _, updateErr := ctrl.client.MachineconfigurationV1().MachineConfigPools().UpdateStatus(context.TODO(), pool, metav1.UpdateOptions{}); updateErr != nil {
564			klog.Errorf("Error updating MachineConfigPool %s: %v", pool.Name, updateErr)
565		}
566		return err
567	}

569	// This function will eventually contain a sane garbage collection policy for rendered MachineConfigs;
570	// see https://github.com/openshift/machine-config-operator/issues/301
571	// It will probably involve making sure we're only GCing a config after all nodes don't have it
572	// in either desired or current config.
573	func (ctrl *Controller) garbageCollectRenderedConfigs(_ *mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool) error {
574		// Temporarily until https://github.com/openshift/machine-config-operator/pull/318
575		// which depends on the strategy for https://github.com/openshift/machine-config-operator/issues/301
576		return nil

578	
579	func (ctrl *Controller) getRenderedMachineConfig(pool *mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool, configs []*mcfgv1.MachineConfig, cc *mcfgv1.ControllerConfig, osImageStreamSet *mcfgv1.OSImageStreamSet) (*mcfgv1.MachineConfig, error) {
580		// If we don't yet have a rendered MachineConfig on the pool, we cannot
581		// perform reconciliation. So we must solely generate the rendered
582		// MachineConfig.
583		if pool.Spec.Configuration.Name == "" {
584			return generateRenderedMachineConfig(pool, configs, cc, osImageStreamSet)
585		}
586	
587		// The pool has a rendered MachineConfig, so we can do more advanced
588		// reconciliation as we generate it.
589		currentMC, err := ctrl.mcLister.Get(pool.Spec.Configuration.Name)
590	
591		// Degenerate case: When the renderedMC that the MCP is currently pointing to is deleted
592		if apierrors.IsNotFound(err) {
593			generated, err := generateRenderedMachineConfig(pool, configs, cc, osImageStreamSet)
594			if err != nil {
595				return nil, err
596			}
597			return generated, nil
598		}
599		if err != nil {
600			return nil, fmt.Errorf("could not get current MachineConfig %s for MachineConfigPool %s: %w", pool.Spec.Configuration.Name, pool.Name, err)
601		}
602	
603		var mcop opv1.MachineConfiguration
604		if ctrl.fgHandler != nil && ctrl.fgHandler.Enabled(features.FeatureGateIrreconcilableMachineConfig) {
605			mcopPtr, err := ctrlcommon.GetIrreconcilableOverrides(ctrl.mcopLister)
606			if err != nil {
607				return nil, err
608			}
609			mcop = *mcopPtr
610		}
611	
612		return generateAndValidateRenderedMachineConfig(currentMC, pool, configs, cc, &mcop.Spec.IrreconcilableValidationOverrides, osImageStreamSet)
613	}
614	
615	// getOSImageStreamNameForPool determines the OS image stream name for a pool,
616	// implementing inheritance from the worker pool for custom pools.
617	// Returns:
618	// - The pool's explicit osImageStream.Name if set
619	// - For custom pools: the worker pool's osImageStream.Name if the custom pool has none
620	// - Empty string (which will resolve to the default stream)
621	func (ctrl *Controller) getOSImageStreamNameForPool(pool *mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool) string {
622		streamName, err := ctrlcommon.GetEffectiveOSImageStreamName(pool, ctrl.mcpLister)
623		if err != nil {
624			klog.V(2).Infof("Failed to get effective osImageStream for pool %s: %v; using default stream", pool.Name, err)
625			return ""
626		}
627	
628		// Log what stream is being used
629		if streamName != "" {
630			if pool.Spec.OSImageStream.Name != "" {
631				klog.V(4).Infof("Pool %s using explicit osImageStream: %s", pool.Name, streamName)
632			} else {
633				klog.V(4).Infof("Custom pool %s inheriting osImageStream from worker: %s", pool.Name, streamName)
634			}
635		} else {
636			if ctrlcommon.IsCustomPool(pool) {
637				klog.V(4).Infof("Custom pool %s using default osImageStream (worker also uses default)", pool.Name)
638			} else {
639				klog.V(4).Infof("Standard pool %s using default osImageStream", pool.Name)
640			}
641		}
642	
643		return streamName
644	}
645	
646	func (ctrl *Controller) getOSImageStreamForPool(pool *mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool) (*mcfgv1.OSImageStreamSet, error) {
647		if !osimagestream.IsFeatureEnabled(ctrl.fgHandler) || ctrl.osImageStreamLister == nil {
648			return nil, nil
649		}
650	
651		// Determine which stream name to use based on inheritance logic
652		streamName := ctrl.getOSImageStreamNameForPool(pool)
653	
654		imageStream, err := ctrl.osImageStreamLister.Get(ctrlcommon.ClusterInstanceNameOSImageStream)
655		if err != nil {
656			return nil, fmt.Errorf("could not get OSImageStream for pool %s: %w", pool.Name, err)
657		}
658	
659		// Guard against using an OSImageStream written by a different operator version.
660		// Unlike ControllerConfig (which has its own generated-by-version guard), the
661		// OSImageStream is fetched independently and directly supplies the OS image URL,
662		// bypassing the ControllerConfig path entirely. Without this check, an old MCC
663		// could render from a new OSImageStream mid-upgrade, producing configs with a new
664		// OS image URL but old config data from the ControllerConfig.
665		streamVersion, ok := imageStream.Annotations[ctrlcommon.ReleaseImageVersionAnnotationKey]
666		if !ok {
667			return nil, fmt.Errorf("ignoring OSImageStream without %s annotation (my version: %s)", ctrlcommon.ReleaseImageVersionAnnotationKey, version.Hash)
668		}
669		if streamVersion != version.Hash {
670			return nil, fmt.Errorf("ignoring OSImageStream with version %s (my version: %s)", streamVersion, version.Hash)
671		}
672	
673		imageStreamSet, err := osimagestream.GetOSImageStreamSetByName(imageStream, streamName)
674		if err != nil {
675			return nil, fmt.Errorf("could not get OSImageStreamSet for pool %s: %w", pool.Name, err)
676		}
677		return imageStreamSet, nil
678	}
679	
680	func (ctrl *Controller) syncGeneratedMachineConfig(pool *mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool, configs []*mcfgv1.MachineConfig) error {
681		if len(configs) == 0 {
682			return nil
683		}
684	
685		cc, err := ctrl.ccLister.Get(ctrlcommon.ControllerConfigName)
686		if err != nil {
687			return err
688		}
689	
690		osImageStreamSet, err := ctrl.getOSImageStreamForPool(pool)
691		if err != nil {
692			return err
693		}
694	
695		generated, err := ctrl.getRenderedMachineConfig(pool, configs, cc, osImageStreamSet)
696		if err != nil {
697			return fmt.Errorf("could not generate rendered MachineConfig: %w", err)
698		}
699	
700		// Validate that the generated MachineConfig does not exceed etcd size limits
701		if err := ctrlcommon.ValidateMachineConfigSize(generated); err != nil {
702			return fmt.Errorf("size validation failed: %w", err)
703		}
704	
705		// Collect metric when OSImageURL was overridden
706		var isOSImageURLOverridden bool
707		if generated.Spec.OSImageURL != ctrlcommon.GetBaseImageContainer(&cc.Spec, osImageStreamSet) {
708			ctrlcommon.OSImageURLOverride.WithLabelValues(pool.Name).Set(1)
709			isOSImageURLOverridden = true
710		} else {
711			// Reset metric when OSImageURL has not been overridden
712			ctrlcommon.OSImageURLOverride.WithLabelValues(pool.Name).Set(0)
713		}
714	
715		source := getMachineConfigRefs(configs)
716	
717		_, err = ctrl.mcLister.Get(generated.Name)
718		if apierrors.IsNotFound(err) {
719			_, err = ctrl.client.MachineconfigurationV1().MachineConfigs().Create(context.TODO(), generated, metav1.CreateOptions{})
720			if err != nil {
721				return err
722			}
723			if isOSImageURLOverridden {
724				ctrl.eventRecorder.Eventf(generated, corev1.EventTypeNormal, "OSImageURLOverridden", "OSImageURL was overridden via machineconfig in %s (was: %s is: %s)", generated.Name, cc.Spec.OSImageURL, generated.Spec.OSImageURL)
725			}
726			klog.V(2).Infof("Generated machineconfig %s from %d configs: %s", generated.Name, len(source), source)
727			ctrl.eventRecorder.Eventf(pool, corev1.EventTypeNormal, "RenderedConfigGenerated", "%s successfully generated (release version: %s, controller version: %s)",
728				generated.Name, generated.Annotations[ctrlcommon.ReleaseImageVersionAnnotationKey], generated.Annotations[ctrlcommon.GeneratedByControllerVersionAnnotationKey])
729		}
730		if err != nil {
731			return err
732		}
733	
734		newPool := pool.DeepCopy()
735		newPool.Spec.Configuration.Source = source
736	
737		if pool.Spec.Configuration.Name == generated.Name {
738			_, _, err = mcoResourceApply.ApplyMachineConfig(ctrl.client.MachineconfigurationV1(), generated)
739			if err != nil {
740				return err
741			}
742			_, err = ctrl.client.MachineconfigurationV1().MachineConfigPools().Update(context.TODO(), newPool, metav1.UpdateOptions{})
743			return err
744		}
745	
746		newPool.Spec.Configuration.Name = generated.Name
747		// TODO(walters) Use subresource or JSON patch, but the latter isn't supported by the unit test mocks
748		pool, err = ctrl.client.MachineconfigurationV1().MachineConfigPools().Update(context.TODO(), newPool, metav1.UpdateOptions{})
749		if err != nil {
750			return err
751		}
752		klog.V(2).Infof("Pool %s: now targeting: %s", pool.Name, pool.Spec.Configuration.Name)
753		ctrlcommon.UpdateStateMetric(ctrlcommon.MCCSubControllerState, "machine-config-controller-render", "Sync Machine Config Pool with new MC", pool.Name)
754		return ctrl.garbageCollectRenderedConfigs(pool)
755	}
756	
757	// generateRenderedMachineConfig takes all MCs for a given pool and returns a single rendered MC. For ex master-XXXX or worker-XXXX
758	func generateRenderedMachineConfig(pool *mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool, configs []*mcfgv1.MachineConfig, cconfig *mcfgv1.ControllerConfig, osImageStreamSet *mcfgv1.OSImageStreamSet) (*mcfgv1.MachineConfig, error) {
759		// Suppress rendered config generation until a corresponding new controller can roll out too.
760		// https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1879099
761		if genver, ok := cconfig.Annotations[daemonconsts.GeneratedByVersionAnnotationKey]; ok {
762			if genver != version.Raw {
763				return nil, fmt.Errorf("ignoring controller config generated from %s (my version: %s)", genver, version.Raw)
764			}
765		} else {
766			return nil, fmt.Errorf("ignoring controller config generated without %s annotation (my version: %s)", daemonconsts.GeneratedByVersionAnnotationKey, version.Raw)
767		}
768	
769		// As an additional check, we should wait until all MCO-owned configs have been regenerated by the newest controller,
770		// before attempting to generate a new MC. See: https://issues.redhat.com/browse/OCPBUGS-3821
771		for _, config := range configs {
772			generatedByControllerVersion := config.Annotations[ctrlcommon.GeneratedByControllerVersionAnnotationKey]
773			if generatedByControllerVersion != "" && generatedByControllerVersion != version.Hash {
774				return nil, fmt.Errorf("ignoring MC %s generated by older version %s (my version: %s)", config.Name, generatedByControllerVersion, version.Hash)
775			}
776		}
777	
778		if cconfig.Spec.BaseOSContainerImage == "" {
779			klog.Warningf("No BaseOSContainerImage set")
780		}
781	
782		// Before merging all MCs for a specific pool, let's make sure MachineConfigs are valid
783		for _, config := range configs {
784			if err := ctrlcommon.ValidateMachineConfig(config.Spec); err != nil {
785				return nil, err
786			}
787		}
788	
789		merged, err := ctrlcommon.MergeMachineConfigs(configs, cconfig, osImageStreamSet)
790	
791		if err != nil {
792			return nil, err
793		}
794		hashedName, err := getMachineConfigHashedName(pool, merged)
795		if err != nil {
796			return nil, err
797		}
798		oref := metav1.NewControllerRef(pool, controllerKind)
799	
800		merged.SetName(hashedName)
801		merged.SetOwnerReferences([]metav1.OwnerReference{*oref})
802		if merged.Annotations == nil {
803			merged.Annotations = map[string]string{}
804		}
805		merged.Annotations[ctrlcommon.GeneratedByControllerVersionAnnotationKey] = version.Hash
806		merged.Annotations[ctrlcommon.ReleaseImageVersionAnnotationKey] = cconfig.Annotations[ctrlcommon.ReleaseImageVersionAnnotationKey]
807	
808		// The operator needs to know the user overrode this, so it knows if it needs to skip the
809		// OSImageURL check during upgrade -- if the user took over managing OS upgrades this way,
810		// the operator shouldn't stop the rest of the upgrade from progressing/completing.
811		if merged.Spec.OSImageURL != ctrlcommon.GetBaseImageContainer(&cconfig.Spec, osImageStreamSet) {
812			merged.Annotations[ctrlcommon.OSImageURLOverriddenKey] = "true"
813			// Log a warning if the osImageURL is set using a tag instead of a digest
814			if !strings.Contains(merged.Spec.OSImageURL, "sha256:") {
815				klog.Warningf("OSImageURL %q for MachineConfig %s is set using a tag instead of a digest. It is highly recommended to use a digest", merged.Spec.OSImageURL, merged.Name)
816			}
817	
818			// If the cluster admin overrides the OSImageURL field, it means they want
819			// to take over managing the OS image. If they then set osImageStream.name,
820			// this implies they want the MCO to manage the OS, which would override
821			// setting OSImageURL. A cluster admin should only be able to do one or the
822			// other; not both.
823			//
824			// In the future, we may want this to also consider some additional
825			// behaviors based on who set OSImageURL.
826			if pool.Spec.OSImageStream.Name != "" {
827				return nil, fmt.Errorf("cannot override MachineConfig osImageURL and set MachineConfigPool spec.osImageStream.name simultaneously")
828			}
829		}
830	
831		return merged, nil
832	}

834	// This function takes into account the current MachineConfig that is present
835	// on the MachineConfigPool. It first attempts to verify that the newly
836	// generated rendered MachineConfig is reconcilable against the current
837	// MachineConfig. If it is not reconcilable, the component MachineConfigs that
838	// were used to generate the rendered MachineConfig will be evaluated
839	// one-by-one to identify which MachineConfig is not reconcilable. The name of
840	// the unreconcilable MachineConfig is included in the returned error.
841	func generateAndValidateRenderedMachineConfig(
842		currentMC *mcfgv1.MachineConfig,
843		pool *mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool,
844		configs []*mcfgv1.MachineConfig,
845		cconfig *mcfgv1.ControllerConfig,
846		validationOverrides *opv1.IrreconcilableValidationOverrides,
847		osImageStreamSet *mcfgv1.OSImageStreamSet) (*mcfgv1.MachineConfig, error) {
848		source := getMachineConfigRefs(configs)
849		klog.V(4).Infof("Considering %d configs %s for MachineConfig generation", len(source), source)
850	
851		generated, err := generateRenderedMachineConfig(pool, configs, cconfig, osImageStreamSet)
852		if err != nil {
853			return nil, err
854		}
855	
856		klog.V(4).Infof("Considering generated MachineConfig %q", generated.Name)
857	
858		if fullErr := ctrlcommon.IsRenderedConfigReconcilable(currentMC, generated, validationOverrides); fullErr != nil {
859			fullMsg := fullErr.Error()
860	
861			// trying to match error reason suffix
862			for _, cfg := range configs {
863				singleErr := ctrlcommon.IsComponentConfigsReconcilable(
864					currentMC, []*mcfgv1.MachineConfig{cfg},
865					validationOverrides,
866				)
867				if singleErr == nil {
868					continue
869				}
870				singleMsg := singleErr.Error()
871	
872				// split off prefix
873				parts := strings.SplitN(singleMsg, ": ", 2)
874				if len(parts) == 2 {
875					reason := parts[1]
876					if strings.Contains(fullMsg, reason) {
877						klog.V(4).Infof("match found for base %q on reason %q", cfg.Name, reason)
878						return nil, fmt.Errorf("reconciliation failed between current config %q and base config %q: %s", currentMC.Name, cfg.Name, reason)
879					}
880				}
881			}
882	
883			// fallback
884			compErr := ctrlcommon.IsComponentConfigsReconcilable(currentMC, configs, validationOverrides)
885			return nil, fmt.Errorf("render reconciliation error: %v; component errors: %v", fullErr, compErr)
886		}
887	
888		klog.V(4).Infof("Rendered MachineConfig %q is reconcilable against %q", generated.Name, currentMC.Name)
889	
890		return generated, nil
891	}

893	// getOSImageStreamNameForPoolBootstrap implements the same inheritance logic as
894	// getOSImageStreamNameForPool but for the bootstrap scenario where we only have
895	// a slice of pools, not a lister.
896	func getOSImageStreamNameForPoolBootstrap(pool *mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool, pools []*mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool) string {
897		// If the pool explicitly sets an osImageStream, use it
898		if pool.Spec.OSImageStream.Name != "" {
899			return pool.Spec.OSImageStream.Name
900		}
901	
902		// Only custom pools inherit from worker
903		if !ctrlcommon.IsCustomPool(pool) {
904			return ""
905		}
906	
907		// Find worker pool in the provided slice
908		for _, p := range pools {
909			if p.Name == ctrlcommon.MachineConfigPoolWorker {
910				return p.Spec.OSImageStream.Name
911			}
912		}
913	
914		// Worker not found, use default
915		return ""
916	}

918	// RunBootstrap runs the render controller in bootstrap mode.
919	// For each pool, it matches the machineconfigs based on label selector and
920	// returns the generated machineconfigs and pool with CurrentMachineConfig status field set.
921	func RunBootstrap(pools []*mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool, configs []*mcfgv1.MachineConfig, cconfig *mcfgv1.ControllerConfig, osImageStream *mcfgv1.OSImageStream) ([]*mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool, []*mcfgv1.MachineConfig, error) {
922		var (
923			opools   []*mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool
924			oconfigs []*mcfgv1.MachineConfig
925		)
926		for _, pool := range pools {
927			pcs, err := getMachineConfigsForPool(pool, configs)
928			if err != nil {
929				return nil, nil, err
930			}
931			var osImageStreamSet *mcfgv1.OSImageStreamSet
932			if osImageStream != nil {
933				// Determine which stream name to use based on inheritance logic
934				streamName := getOSImageStreamNameForPoolBootstrap(pool, pools)
935				osImageStreamSet, err = osimagestream.GetOSImageStreamSetByName(osImageStream, streamName)
936				if err != nil {
937					return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("couldn't get the OSImageStream for pool %s %w", pool.Name, err)
938				}
939			}
940	
941			generated, err := generateRenderedMachineConfig(pool, pcs, cconfig, osImageStreamSet)
942			if err != nil {
943				return nil, nil, err
944			}
945	
946			source := getMachineConfigRefs(configs)
947	
948			pool.Spec.Configuration.Name = generated.Name
949			pool.Spec.Configuration.Source = source
950			pool.Status.Configuration.Name = generated.Name
951			pool.Status.Configuration.Source = source
952			opools = append(opools, pool)
953			oconfigs = append(oconfigs, generated)
954		}
955		return opools, oconfigs, nil
956	}

958	// getMachineConfigsForPool is called by RunBootstrap and returns configs that match label from configs for a pool.
959	func getMachineConfigsForPool(pool *mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool, configs []*mcfgv1.MachineConfig) ([]*mcfgv1.MachineConfig, error) {
960		selector, err := metav1.LabelSelectorAsSelector(pool.Spec.MachineConfigSelector)
961		if err != nil {
962			return nil, fmt.Errorf("invalid label selector: %w", err)
963		}
964	
965		var out []*mcfgv1.MachineConfig
966	
967		// If a pool with a nil or empty selector creeps in, it should match nothing
968		if selector.Empty() {
969			return out, nil
970		}
971		for idx, config := range configs {
972			if selector.Matches(labels.Set(config.Labels)) {
973				out = append(out, configs[idx])
974			}
975		}
976		if len(out) == 0 {
977			return nil, fmt.Errorf("couldn't find any MachineConfigs for pool: %v", pool.Name)
978		}
979		return out, nil
980	}

41	const (
42		// maxRetries is the number of times a machineconfig pool will be retried before it is dropped out of the queue.
43		// With the current rate-limiter in use (5ms*2^(maxRetries-1)) the following numbers represent the times
44		// a machineconfig pool is going to be requeued:
45		//
46		// 5ms, 10ms, 20ms, 40ms, 80ms, 160ms, 320ms, 640ms, 1.3s, 2.6s, 5.1s, 10.2s, 20.4s, 41s, 82s
47		maxRetries = 15
48	
49		// renderDelay is a pause to avoid churn in MachineConfigs; see
50		// https://github.com/openshift/machine-config-operator/issues/301
51		renderDelay = 5 * time.Second
52	)
53	
54	var (
55		// controllerKind contains the schema.GroupVersionKind for this controller type.
56		controllerKind = mcfgv1.SchemeGroupVersion.WithKind("MachineConfigPool")
57	
58		machineconfigKind = mcfgv1.SchemeGroupVersion.WithKind("MachineConfig")
59	)

61	// Controller defines the render controller.
62	type Controller struct {
63		client        mcfgclientset.Interface
64		eventRecorder record.EventRecorder
65	
66		syncHandler              func(mcp string) error
67		enqueueMachineConfigPool func(*mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool)
68	
69		mcpLister           mcfglistersv1.MachineConfigPoolLister
70		mcLister            mcfglistersv1.MachineConfigLister
71		osImageStreamLister mcfglistersv1.OSImageStreamLister
72	
73		mcpListerSynced           cache.InformerSynced
74		mcListerSynced            cache.InformerSynced
75		osImageStreamListerSynced cache.InformerSynced
76	
77		ccLister       mcfglistersv1.ControllerConfigLister
78		ccListerSynced cache.InformerSynced
79	
80		crcLister       mcfglistersv1.ContainerRuntimeConfigLister
81		crcListerSynced cache.InformerSynced
82	
83		mckLister       mcfglistersv1.KubeletConfigLister
84		mckListerSynced cache.InformerSynced
85	
86		mcopLister       mcoplistersv1.MachineConfigurationLister
87		mcopListerSynced cache.InformerSynced
88	
89		fgHandler ctrlcommon.FeatureGatesHandler
90	
91		queue workqueue.TypedRateLimitingInterface[string]
92	}
93	
94	// New returns a new render controller.
95	func New(
96		mcpInformer mcfginformersv1.MachineConfigPoolInformer,
97		mcInformer mcfginformersv1.MachineConfigInformer,
98		ccInformer mcfginformersv1.ControllerConfigInformer,
99		crcInformer mcfginformersv1.ContainerRuntimeConfigInformer,
100		mckInformer mcfginformersv1.KubeletConfigInformer,
101		mcopInformer mcopinformersv1.MachineConfigurationInformer,
102		osImageStreamInformer mcfginformersv1.OSImageStreamInformer,
103		kubeClient clientset.Interface,
104		mcfgClient mcfgclientset.Interface,
105		featureGatesHandler ctrlcommon.FeatureGatesHandler,
106	) *Controller {
107		eventBroadcaster := record.NewBroadcaster()
108		eventBroadcaster.StartLogging(klog.Infof)
109		eventBroadcaster.StartRecordingToSink(&corev1client.EventSinkImpl{Interface: kubeClient.CoreV1().Events("")})
110	
111		ctrl := &Controller{
112			client:        mcfgClient,
113			eventRecorder: ctrlcommon.NamespacedEventRecorder(eventBroadcaster.NewRecorder(scheme.Scheme, corev1.EventSource{Component: "machineconfigcontroller-rendercontroller"})),
114			queue: workqueue.NewTypedRateLimitingQueueWithConfig(
115				workqueue.DefaultTypedControllerRateLimiter[string](),
116				workqueue.TypedRateLimitingQueueConfig[string]{Name: "machineconfigcontroller-rendercontroller"}),
117			fgHandler: featureGatesHandler,
118		}
119	
120		mcpInformer.Informer().AddEventHandler(cache.ResourceEventHandlerFuncs{
121			AddFunc:    ctrl.addMachineConfigPool,
122			UpdateFunc: ctrl.updateMachineConfigPool,
123			DeleteFunc: ctrl.deleteMachineConfigPool,
124		})
125		mcInformer.Informer().AddEventHandler(cache.ResourceEventHandlerFuncs{
126			AddFunc:    ctrl.addMachineConfig,
127			UpdateFunc: ctrl.updateMachineConfig,
128			DeleteFunc: ctrl.deleteMachineConfig,
129		})
130	
131		ctrl.syncHandler = ctrl.syncMachineConfigPool
132		ctrl.enqueueMachineConfigPool = ctrl.enqueueDefault
133	
134		ctrl.mcpLister = mcpInformer.Lister()
135		ctrl.mcLister = mcInformer.Lister()
136		ctrl.mcpListerSynced = mcpInformer.Informer().HasSynced
137		ctrl.mcListerSynced = mcInformer.Informer().HasSynced
138		ctrl.ccLister = ccInformer.Lister()
139		ctrl.ccListerSynced = ccInformer.Informer().HasSynced
140		ctrl.crcLister = crcInformer.Lister()
141		ctrl.crcListerSynced = crcInformer.Informer().HasSynced
142		ctrl.mckLister = mckInformer.Lister()
143		ctrl.mckListerSynced = mckInformer.Informer().HasSynced
144		ctrl.mcopLister = mcopInformer.Lister()
145		ctrl.mcopListerSynced = mcopInformer.Informer().HasSynced
146	
147		if osImageStreamInformer != nil && osimagestream.IsFeatureEnabled(ctrl.fgHandler) {
148			ctrl.osImageStreamLister = osImageStreamInformer.Lister()
149			ctrl.osImageStreamListerSynced = osImageStreamInformer.Informer().HasSynced
150			osImageStreamInformer.Informer().AddEventHandler(
151				cache.ResourceEventHandlerFuncs{UpdateFunc: ctrl.updateOSImageStream},
152			)
153		}
154		return ctrl
155	}
156	
157	// Run executes the render controller.
158	func (ctrl *Controller) Run(workers int, stopCh <-chan struct{}) {
159		defer utilruntime.HandleCrash()
160		defer ctrl.queue.ShutDown()
161	
162		listerCaches := []cache.InformerSynced{ctrl.mcpListerSynced, ctrl.mcListerSynced, ctrl.ccListerSynced}
163	
164		// OSImageStreams and MCPs fetched only if FeatureGateOSStreams active
165		if ctrl.osImageStreamListerSynced != nil {
166			listerCaches = append(listerCaches, ctrl.osImageStreamListerSynced)
167		}
168		if !cache.WaitForCacheSync(stopCh, listerCaches...) {
169			return
170		}
171	
172		klog.Info("Starting MachineConfigController-RenderController")
173		defer klog.Info("Shutting down MachineConfigController-RenderController")
174	
175		for i := 0; i < workers; i++ {
176			go wait.Until(ctrl.worker, time.Second, stopCh)
177		}
178	
179		<-stopCh
180	}
181	
182	func (ctrl *Controller) addMachineConfigPool(obj interface{}) {
183		pool := obj.(*mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool)
184		klog.V(4).Infof("Adding MachineConfigPool %s", pool.Name)
185		ctrl.enqueueMachineConfigPool(pool)
186	
187	}
188	
189	func (ctrl *Controller) updateMachineConfigPool(old, cur interface{}) {
190		oldPool := old.(*mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool)
191		curPool := cur.(*mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool)
192	
193		klog.V(4).Infof("Updating MachineConfigPool %s", oldPool.Name)
194		ctrl.enqueueMachineConfigPool(curPool)
195	
196		// If the worker pool's osImageStream changed, enqueue all custom pools that might inherit from it
197		if curPool.Name == ctrlcommon.MachineConfigPoolWorker &&
198			oldPool.Spec.OSImageStream.Name != curPool.Spec.OSImageStream.Name {
199			klog.V(4).Infof("Worker pool osImageStream changed from %q to %q, enqueuing custom pools",
200				oldPool.Spec.OSImageStream.Name, curPool.Spec.OSImageStream.Name)
201			ctrl.enqueueCustomPools()
202		}
203	}
204	
205	func (ctrl *Controller) deleteMachineConfigPool(obj interface{}) {
206		pool, ok := obj.(*mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool)
207		if !ok {
208			tombstone, ok := obj.(cache.DeletedFinalStateUnknown)
209			if !ok {
210				utilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf("couldn't get object from tombstone %#v", obj))
211				return
212			}
213			pool, ok = tombstone.Obj.(*mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool)
214			if !ok {
215				utilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf("tombstone contained object that is not a MachineConfigPool %#v", obj))
216				return
217			}
218		}
219		klog.V(4).Infof("Deleting MachineConfigPool %s", pool.Name)
220		// TODO(abhinavdahiya): handle deletes.
221	}
222	
223	func (ctrl *Controller) addMachineConfig(obj interface{}) {
224		mc := obj.(*mcfgv1.MachineConfig)
225		if mc.DeletionTimestamp != nil {
226			ctrl.deleteMachineConfig(mc)
227			return
228		}
229	
230		controllerRef := metav1.GetControllerOf(mc)
231		if controllerRef != nil {
232			if pool := ctrl.resolveControllerRef(controllerRef); pool != nil {
233				klog.V(4).Infof("MachineConfig %s added", mc.Name)
234				ctrl.enqueueMachineConfigPool(pool)
235				return
236			}
237		}
238	
239		pools, err := ctrl.getPoolsForMachineConfig(mc)
240		if err != nil {
241			klog.Errorf("error finding pools for machineconfig: %v", err)
242			return
243		}
244	
245		klog.V(4).Infof("MachineConfig %s added", mc.Name)
246		for _, p := range pools {
247			ctrl.enqueueMachineConfigPool(p)
248		}
249	}
250	
251	func (ctrl *Controller) updateMachineConfig(old, cur interface{}) {
252		oldMC := old.(*mcfgv1.MachineConfig)
253		curMC := cur.(*mcfgv1.MachineConfig)
254	
255		// return if spec hasn't changed
256		if reflect.DeepEqual(oldMC.Spec, curMC.Spec) {
257			return
258		}
259		klog.V(4).Infof("MachineConfig %s updated", curMC.Name)
260	
261		curControllerRef := metav1.GetControllerOf(curMC)
262		oldControllerRef := metav1.GetControllerOf(oldMC)
263		controllerRefChanged := !reflect.DeepEqual(curControllerRef, oldControllerRef)
264		if controllerRefChanged && oldControllerRef != nil {
265			klog.Errorf("machineconfig has changed controller, not allowed.")
266			return
267		}
268	
269		if curControllerRef != nil {
270			if pool := ctrl.resolveControllerRef(curControllerRef); pool != nil {
271				ctrl.enqueueMachineConfigPool(pool)
272				return
273			}
274		}
275	
276		pools, err := ctrl.getPoolsForMachineConfig(curMC)
277		if err != nil {
278			klog.Errorf("error finding pools for machineconfig: %v", err)
279			return
280		}
281		for _, p := range pools {
282			ctrl.enqueueMachineConfigPool(p)
283		}
284	}
285	
286	func (ctrl *Controller) updateOSImageStream(old, cur interface{}) {
287		oldOSImageStream := old.(*mcfgv1.OSImageStream)
288		curOSImageStream := cur.(*mcfgv1.OSImageStream)
289	
290		// return if status hasn't changed
291		// The operator components only reacts to what the OSImageStream
292		// publish in its status subresource.
293		if reflect.DeepEqual(oldOSImageStream.Status, curOSImageStream.Status) {
294			return
295		}
296		klog.V(4).Info("OSImageStream updated")
297	
298		pList, err := ctrl.mcpLister.List(labels.Everything())
299		if err != nil {
300			klog.Errorf("error listing MCPs for OSImageStream update: %v", err)
301		}
302	
303		for _, p := range pList {
304			ctrl.enqueueMachineConfigPool(p)
305		}
306	}
307	
308	func (ctrl *Controller) deleteMachineConfig(obj interface{}) {
309		mc, ok := obj.(*mcfgv1.MachineConfig)
310	
311		if !ok {
312			tombstone, ok := obj.(cache.DeletedFinalStateUnknown)
313			if !ok {
314				utilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf("couldn't get object from tombstone %#v", obj))
315				return
316			}
317			mc, ok = tombstone.Obj.(*mcfgv1.MachineConfig)
318			if !ok {
319				utilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf("tombstone contained object that is not a MachineConfig %#v", obj))
320				return
321			}
322		}
323	
324		controllerRef := metav1.GetControllerOf(mc)
325		if controllerRef != nil {
326			if pool := ctrl.resolveControllerRef(controllerRef); pool != nil {
327				klog.V(4).Infof("MachineConfig %s deleted", mc.Name)
328				ctrl.enqueueMachineConfigPool(pool)
329				return
330			}
331		}
332	
333		pools, err := ctrl.getPoolsForMachineConfig(mc)
334		if err != nil {
335			klog.Errorf("error finding pools for machineconfig: %v", err)
336			return
337		}
338	
339		klog.V(4).Infof("MachineConfig %s deleted", mc.Name)
340		for _, p := range pools {
341			ctrl.enqueueMachineConfigPool(p)
342		}
343	}
344	
345	func (ctrl *Controller) resolveControllerRef(controllerRef *metav1.OwnerReference) *mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool {
346		// We can't look up by UID, so look up by Name and then verify UID.
347		// Don't even try to look up by Name if it's the wrong Kind.
348		if controllerRef.Kind != controllerKind.Kind {
349			return nil
350		}
351		pool, err := ctrl.mcpLister.Get(controllerRef.Name)
352		if err != nil {
353			return nil
354		}
355	
356		if pool.UID != controllerRef.UID {
357			// The controller we found with this Name is not the same one that the
358			// ControllerRef points to.
359			return nil
360		}
361		return pool
362	}
363	
364	func (ctrl *Controller) getPoolsForMachineConfig(config *mcfgv1.MachineConfig) ([]*mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool, error) {
365		if len(config.Labels) == 0 {
366			return nil, fmt.Errorf("no MachineConfigPool found for MachineConfig %v because it has no labels", config.Name)
367		}
368	
369		pList, err := ctrl.mcpLister.List(labels.Everything())
370		if err != nil {
371			return nil, err
372		}
373	
374		var pools []*mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool
375		for _, p := range pList {
376			selector, err := metav1.LabelSelectorAsSelector(p.Spec.MachineConfigSelector)
377			if err != nil {
378				return nil, fmt.Errorf("invalid label selector: %w", err)
379			}
380	
381			// If a pool with a nil or empty selector creeps in, it should match nothing, not everything.
382			if selector.Empty() || !selector.Matches(labels.Set(config.Labels)) {
383				continue
384			}
385	
386			pools = append(pools, p)
387		}
388	
389		if len(pools) == 0 {
390			return nil, fmt.Errorf("could not find any MachineConfigPool set for MachineConfig %s with labels: %v", config.Name, config.Labels)
391		}
392		return pools, nil
393	}
394	
395	func (ctrl *Controller) enqueue(pool *mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool) {
396		key, err := cache.DeletionHandlingMetaNamespaceKeyFunc(pool)
397		if err != nil {
398			utilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf("couldn't get key for object %#v: %w", pool, err))
399			return
400		}
401	
402		ctrl.queue.Add(key)
403	}
404	
405	func (ctrl *Controller) enqueueRateLimited(pool *mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool) {
406		key, err := cache.DeletionHandlingMetaNamespaceKeyFunc(pool)
407		if err != nil {
408			utilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf("couldn't get key for object %#v: %w", pool, err))
409			return
410		}
411	
412		ctrl.queue.AddRateLimited(key)
413	}
414	
415	// enqueueAfter will enqueue a pool after the provided amount of time.
416	func (ctrl *Controller) enqueueAfter(pool *mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool, after time.Duration) {
417		key, err := cache.DeletionHandlingMetaNamespaceKeyFunc(pool)
418		if err != nil {
419			utilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf("couldn't get key for object %#v: %w", pool, err))
420			return
421		}
422	
423		ctrl.queue.AddAfter(key, after)
424	}
425	
426	// enqueueDefault calls a default enqueue function
427	func (ctrl *Controller) enqueueDefault(pool *mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool) {
428		ctrl.enqueueAfter(pool, renderDelay)
429	}
430	
431	// enqueueCustomPools enqueues all custom pools (pools that are not master, worker, or arbiter).
432	// This is used when the worker pool changes in a way that might affect custom pools that inherit from it.
433	func (ctrl *Controller) enqueueCustomPools() {
434		pools, err := ctrl.mcpLister.List(labels.Everything())
435		if err != nil {
436			klog.Warningf("Failed to list pools when enqueuing custom pools: %v", err)
437			return
438		}
439	
440		for _, pool := range pools {
441			if ctrlcommon.IsCustomPool(pool) {
442				klog.V(4).Infof("Enqueuing custom pool %s due to worker pool change", pool.Name)
443				ctrl.enqueueMachineConfigPool(pool)
444			}
445		}
446	}
447	
448	// worker runs a worker thread that just dequeues items, processes them, and marks them done.
449	// It enforces that the syncHandler is never invoked concurrently with the same key.
450	func (ctrl *Controller) worker() {
451		for ctrl.processNextWorkItem() {
452		}
453	}
454	
455	func (ctrl *Controller) processNextWorkItem() bool {
456		key, quit := ctrl.queue.Get()
457		if quit {
458			return false
459		}
460		defer ctrl.queue.Done(key)
461	
462		err := ctrl.syncHandler(key)
463		ctrl.handleErr(err, key)
464	
465		return true
466	}
467	
468	func (ctrl *Controller) handleErr(err error, key string) {
469		if err == nil {
470			ctrl.queue.Forget(key)
471			return
472		}
473	
474		if ctrl.queue.NumRequeues(key) < maxRetries {
475			klog.V(2).Infof("Error syncing machineconfigpool %v: %v", key, err)
476			ctrl.queue.AddRateLimited(key)
477			return
478		}
479	
480		utilruntime.HandleError(err)
481		klog.V(2).Infof("Dropping machineconfigpool %q out of the queue: %v", key, err)
482		ctrl.queue.Forget(key)
483		ctrl.queue.AddAfter(key, 1*time.Minute)
484	}
485	
486	// syncMachineConfigPool will sync the machineconfig pool with the given key.
487	// This function is not meant to be invoked concurrently with the same key.
488	func (ctrl *Controller) syncMachineConfigPool(key string) error {
489		startTime := time.Now()
490		klog.V(4).Infof("Started syncing machineconfigpool %q (%v)", key, startTime)
491		defer func() {
492			klog.V(4).Infof("Finished syncing machineconfigpool %q (%v)", key, time.Since(startTime))
493		}()
494	
495		_, name, err := cache.SplitMetaNamespaceKey(key)
496		if err != nil {
497			return err
498		}
499		machineconfigpool, err := ctrl.mcpLister.Get(name)
500		if errors.IsNotFound(err) {
501			klog.V(2).Infof("MachineConfigPool %v has been deleted", key)
502			return nil
503		}
504		if err != nil {
505			return err
506		}
507	
508		// Deep-copy otherwise we are mutating our cache.
509		// TODO: Deep-copy only when needed.
510		pool := machineconfigpool.DeepCopy()
511		everything := metav1.LabelSelector{}
512		if reflect.DeepEqual(pool.Spec.MachineConfigSelector, &everything) {
513			ctrl.eventRecorder.Eventf(pool, corev1.EventTypeWarning, "SelectingAll", "This machineconfigpool is selecting all machineconfigs. A non-empty selector is required.")
514			return nil
515		}
516	
517		selector, err := metav1.LabelSelectorAsSelector(pool.Spec.MachineConfigSelector)
518		if err != nil {
519			return err
520		}
521	
522		// TODO(runcom): add tests in render_controller_test.go for this condition
523		if err := apihelpers.IsControllerConfigCompleted(ctrlcommon.ControllerConfigName, ctrl.ccLister.Get); err != nil {
524			return err
525		}
526	
527		if err := apihelpers.AreMCGeneratingSubControllersCompletedForPool(ctrl.crcLister.List, ctrl.mckLister.List, pool.Labels); err != nil {
528			return err
529		}
530	
531		mcs, err := ctrl.mcLister.List(selector)
532		if err != nil {
533			return err
534		}
535		sort.SliceStable(mcs, func(i, j int) bool { return mcs[i].Name < mcs[j].Name })
536	
537		if len(mcs) == 0 {
538			return ctrl.syncFailingStatus(pool, fmt.Errorf("no MachineConfigs found matching selector %v", selector))
539		}
540	
541		if err := ctrl.syncGeneratedMachineConfig(pool, mcs); err != nil {
542			klog.Errorf("Error syncing Generated MCFG: %v", err)
543			return ctrl.syncFailingStatus(pool, err)
544		}
545		return ctrl.syncAvailableStatus(pool)
546	}
547	
548	func (ctrl *Controller) syncAvailableStatus(pool *mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool) error {
549		if apihelpers.IsMachineConfigPoolConditionFalse(pool.Status.Conditions, mcfgv1.MachineConfigPoolRenderDegraded) {
550			return nil
551		}
552		sdegraded := apihelpers.NewMachineConfigPoolCondition(mcfgv1.MachineConfigPoolRenderDegraded, corev1.ConditionFalse, "", "")
553		apihelpers.SetMachineConfigPoolCondition(&pool.Status, *sdegraded)
554		if _, err := ctrl.client.MachineconfigurationV1().MachineConfigPools().UpdateStatus(context.TODO(), pool, metav1.UpdateOptions{}); err != nil {
555			return err
556		}
557		return nil
558	}
559	
560	func (ctrl *Controller) syncFailingStatus(pool *mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool, err error) error {
561		sdegraded := apihelpers.NewMachineConfigPoolCondition(mcfgv1.MachineConfigPoolRenderDegraded, corev1.ConditionTrue, "", fmt.Sprintf("Failed to render configuration for pool %s: %v", pool.Name, err))
562		apihelpers.SetMachineConfigPoolCondition(&pool.Status, *sdegraded)
563		if _, updateErr := ctrl.client.MachineconfigurationV1().MachineConfigPools().UpdateStatus(context.TODO(), pool, metav1.UpdateOptions{}); updateErr != nil {
564			klog.Errorf("Error updating MachineConfigPool %s: %v", pool.Name, updateErr)
565		}
566		return err
567	}
568	
569	// This function will eventually contain a sane garbage collection policy for rendered MachineConfigs;
570	// see https://github.com/openshift/machine-config-operator/issues/301
571	// It will probably involve making sure we're only GCing a config after all nodes don't have it

579	func (ctrl *Controller) getRenderedMachineConfig(pool *mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool, configs []*mcfgv1.MachineConfig, cc *mcfgv1.ControllerConfig, osImageStreamSet *mcfgv1.OSImageStreamSet) (*mcfgv1.MachineConfig, error) {
580		// If we don't yet have a rendered MachineConfig on the pool, we cannot
581		// perform reconciliation. So we must solely generate the rendered
582		// MachineConfig.
583		if pool.Spec.Configuration.Name == "" {
584			return generateRenderedMachineConfig(pool, configs, cc, osImageStreamSet)
585		}
586	
587		// The pool has a rendered MachineConfig, so we can do more advanced
588		// reconciliation as we generate it.
589		currentMC, err := ctrl.mcLister.Get(pool.Spec.Configuration.Name)
590	
591		// Degenerate case: When the renderedMC that the MCP is currently pointing to is deleted
592		if apierrors.IsNotFound(err) {
593			generated, err := generateRenderedMachineConfig(pool, configs, cc, osImageStreamSet)
594			if err != nil {
595				return nil, err
596			}
597			return generated, nil
598		}
599		if err != nil {
600			return nil, fmt.Errorf("could not get current MachineConfig %s for MachineConfigPool %s: %w", pool.Spec.Configuration.Name, pool.Name, err)
601		}
602	
603		var mcop opv1.MachineConfiguration
604		if ctrl.fgHandler != nil && ctrl.fgHandler.Enabled(features.FeatureGateIrreconcilableMachineConfig) {
605			mcopPtr, err := ctrlcommon.GetIrreconcilableOverrides(ctrl.mcopLister)
606			if err != nil {
607				return nil, err
608			}
609			mcop = *mcopPtr
610		}
611	
612		return generateAndValidateRenderedMachineConfig(currentMC, pool, configs, cc, &mcop.Spec.IrreconcilableValidationOverrides, osImageStreamSet)
613	}

615	// getOSImageStreamNameForPool determines the OS image stream name for a pool,
616	// implementing inheritance from the worker pool for custom pools.
617	// Returns:
618	// - The pool's explicit osImageStream.Name if set
619	// - For custom pools: the worker pool's osImageStream.Name if the custom pool has none
620	// - Empty string (which will resolve to the default stream)
621	func (ctrl *Controller) getOSImageStreamNameForPool(pool *mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool) string {
622		streamName, err := ctrlcommon.GetEffectiveOSImageStreamName(pool, ctrl.mcpLister)
623		if err != nil {
624			klog.V(2).Infof("Failed to get effective osImageStream for pool %s: %v; using default stream", pool.Name, err)
625			return ""
626		}
627	
628		// Log what stream is being used
629		if streamName != "" {
630			if pool.Spec.OSImageStream.Name != "" {
631				klog.V(4).Infof("Pool %s using explicit osImageStream: %s", pool.Name, streamName)
632			} else {
633				klog.V(4).Infof("Custom pool %s inheriting osImageStream from worker: %s", pool.Name, streamName)
634			}
635		} else {
636			if ctrlcommon.IsCustomPool(pool) {
637				klog.V(4).Infof("Custom pool %s using default osImageStream (worker also uses default)", pool.Name)
638			} else {
639				klog.V(4).Infof("Standard pool %s using default osImageStream", pool.Name)
640			}
641		}
642	
643		return streamName
644	}

646	func (ctrl *Controller) getOSImageStreamForPool(pool *mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool) (*mcfgv1.OSImageStreamSet, error) {
647		if !osimagestream.IsFeatureEnabled(ctrl.fgHandler) || ctrl.osImageStreamLister == nil {
648			return nil, nil
649		}
650	
651		// Determine which stream name to use based on inheritance logic
652		streamName := ctrl.getOSImageStreamNameForPool(pool)
653	
654		imageStream, err := ctrl.osImageStreamLister.Get(ctrlcommon.ClusterInstanceNameOSImageStream)
655		if err != nil {
656			return nil, fmt.Errorf("could not get OSImageStream for pool %s: %w", pool.Name, err)
657		}
658	
659		// Guard against using an OSImageStream written by a different operator version.
660		// Unlike ControllerConfig (which has its own generated-by-version guard), the
661		// OSImageStream is fetched independently and directly supplies the OS image URL,
662		// bypassing the ControllerConfig path entirely. Without this check, an old MCC
663		// could render from a new OSImageStream mid-upgrade, producing configs with a new
664		// OS image URL but old config data from the ControllerConfig.
665		streamVersion, ok := imageStream.Annotations[ctrlcommon.ReleaseImageVersionAnnotationKey]
666		if !ok {
667			return nil, fmt.Errorf("ignoring OSImageStream without %s annotation (my version: %s)", ctrlcommon.ReleaseImageVersionAnnotationKey, version.Hash)
668		}
669		if streamVersion != version.Hash {
670			return nil, fmt.Errorf("ignoring OSImageStream with version %s (my version: %s)", streamVersion, version.Hash)
671		}
672	
673		imageStreamSet, err := osimagestream.GetOSImageStreamSetByName(imageStream, streamName)
674		if err != nil {
675			return nil, fmt.Errorf("could not get OSImageStreamSet for pool %s: %w", pool.Name, err)
676		}
677		return imageStreamSet, nil
678	}

680	func (ctrl *Controller) syncGeneratedMachineConfig(pool *mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool, configs []*mcfgv1.MachineConfig) error {
681		if len(configs) == 0 {
682			return nil
683		}
684	
685		cc, err := ctrl.ccLister.Get(ctrlcommon.ControllerConfigName)
686		if err != nil {
687			return err
688		}
689	
690		osImageStreamSet, err := ctrl.getOSImageStreamForPool(pool)
691		if err != nil {
692			return err
693		}
694	
695		generated, err := ctrl.getRenderedMachineConfig(pool, configs, cc, osImageStreamSet)
696		if err != nil {
697			return fmt.Errorf("could not generate rendered MachineConfig: %w", err)
698		}
699	
700		// Validate that the generated MachineConfig does not exceed etcd size limits
701		if err := ctrlcommon.ValidateMachineConfigSize(generated); err != nil {
702			return fmt.Errorf("size validation failed: %w", err)
703		}
704	
705		// Collect metric when OSImageURL was overridden
706		var isOSImageURLOverridden bool
707		if generated.Spec.OSImageURL != ctrlcommon.GetBaseImageContainer(&cc.Spec, osImageStreamSet) {
708			ctrlcommon.OSImageURLOverride.WithLabelValues(pool.Name).Set(1)
709			isOSImageURLOverridden = true
710		} else {
711			// Reset metric when OSImageURL has not been overridden
712			ctrlcommon.OSImageURLOverride.WithLabelValues(pool.Name).Set(0)
713		}
714	
715		source := getMachineConfigRefs(configs)
716	
717		_, err = ctrl.mcLister.Get(generated.Name)
718		if apierrors.IsNotFound(err) {
719			_, err = ctrl.client.MachineconfigurationV1().MachineConfigs().Create(context.TODO(), generated, metav1.CreateOptions{})
720			if err != nil {
721				return err
722			}
723			if isOSImageURLOverridden {
724				ctrl.eventRecorder.Eventf(generated, corev1.EventTypeNormal, "OSImageURLOverridden", "OSImageURL was overridden via machineconfig in %s (was: %s is: %s)", generated.Name, cc.Spec.OSImageURL, generated.Spec.OSImageURL)
725			}
726			klog.V(2).Infof("Generated machineconfig %s from %d configs: %s", generated.Name, len(source), source)
727			ctrl.eventRecorder.Eventf(pool, corev1.EventTypeNormal, "RenderedConfigGenerated", "%s successfully generated (release version: %s, controller version: %s)",
728				generated.Name, generated.Annotations[ctrlcommon.ReleaseImageVersionAnnotationKey], generated.Annotations[ctrlcommon.GeneratedByControllerVersionAnnotationKey])
729		}
730		if err != nil {
731			return err
732		}
733	
734		newPool := pool.DeepCopy()
735		newPool.Spec.Configuration.Source = source
736	
737		if pool.Spec.Configuration.Name == generated.Name {
738			_, _, err = mcoResourceApply.ApplyMachineConfig(ctrl.client.MachineconfigurationV1(), generated)
739			if err != nil {
740				return err
741			}
742			_, err = ctrl.client.MachineconfigurationV1().MachineConfigPools().Update(context.TODO(), newPool, metav1.UpdateOptions{})
743			return err
744		}
745	
746		newPool.Spec.Configuration.Name = generated.Name
747		// TODO(walters) Use subresource or JSON patch, but the latter isn't supported by the unit test mocks
748		pool, err = ctrl.client.MachineconfigurationV1().MachineConfigPools().Update(context.TODO(), newPool, metav1.UpdateOptions{})
749		if err != nil {
750			return err
751		}
752		klog.V(2).Infof("Pool %s: now targeting: %s", pool.Name, pool.Spec.Configuration.Name)
753		ctrlcommon.UpdateStateMetric(ctrlcommon.MCCSubControllerState, "machine-config-controller-render", "Sync Machine Config Pool with new MC", pool.Name)
754		return ctrl.garbageCollectRenderedConfigs(pool)
755	}

757	// generateRenderedMachineConfig takes all MCs for a given pool and returns a single rendered MC. For ex master-XXXX or worker-XXXX
758	func generateRenderedMachineConfig(pool *mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool, configs []*mcfgv1.MachineConfig, cconfig *mcfgv1.ControllerConfig, osImageStreamSet *mcfgv1.OSImageStreamSet) (*mcfgv1.MachineConfig, error) {
759		// Suppress rendered config generation until a corresponding new controller can roll out too.
760		// https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1879099
761		if genver, ok := cconfig.Annotations[daemonconsts.GeneratedByVersionAnnotationKey]; ok {
762			if genver != version.Raw {
763				return nil, fmt.Errorf("ignoring controller config generated from %s (my version: %s)", genver, version.Raw)
764			}
765		} else {
766			return nil, fmt.Errorf("ignoring controller config generated without %s annotation (my version: %s)", daemonconsts.GeneratedByVersionAnnotationKey, version.Raw)
767		}
768	
769		// As an additional check, we should wait until all MCO-owned configs have been regenerated by the newest controller,
770		// before attempting to generate a new MC. See: https://issues.redhat.com/browse/OCPBUGS-3821
771		for _, config := range configs {
772			generatedByControllerVersion := config.Annotations[ctrlcommon.GeneratedByControllerVersionAnnotationKey]
773			if generatedByControllerVersion != "" && generatedByControllerVersion != version.Hash {
774				return nil, fmt.Errorf("ignoring MC %s generated by older version %s (my version: %s)", config.Name, generatedByControllerVersion, version.Hash)
775			}
776		}
777	
778		if cconfig.Spec.BaseOSContainerImage == "" {
779			klog.Warningf("No BaseOSContainerImage set")
780		}
781	
782		// Before merging all MCs for a specific pool, let's make sure MachineConfigs are valid
783		for _, config := range configs {
784			if err := ctrlcommon.ValidateMachineConfig(config.Spec); err != nil {
785				return nil, err
786			}
787		}
788	
789		merged, err := ctrlcommon.MergeMachineConfigs(configs, cconfig, osImageStreamSet)
790	
791		if err != nil {
792			return nil, err
793		}
794		hashedName, err := getMachineConfigHashedName(pool, merged)
795		if err != nil {
796			return nil, err
797		}
798		oref := metav1.NewControllerRef(pool, controllerKind)
799	
800		merged.SetName(hashedName)
801		merged.SetOwnerReferences([]metav1.OwnerReference{*oref})
802		if merged.Annotations == nil {
803			merged.Annotations = map[string]string{}
804		}
805		merged.Annotations[ctrlcommon.GeneratedByControllerVersionAnnotationKey] = version.Hash
806		merged.Annotations[ctrlcommon.ReleaseImageVersionAnnotationKey] = cconfig.Annotations[ctrlcommon.ReleaseImageVersionAnnotationKey]
807	
808		// The operator needs to know the user overrode this, so it knows if it needs to skip the
809		// OSImageURL check during upgrade -- if the user took over managing OS upgrades this way,
810		// the operator shouldn't stop the rest of the upgrade from progressing/completing.
811		if merged.Spec.OSImageURL != ctrlcommon.GetBaseImageContainer(&cconfig.Spec, osImageStreamSet) {
812			merged.Annotations[ctrlcommon.OSImageURLOverriddenKey] = "true"
813			// Log a warning if the osImageURL is set using a tag instead of a digest
814			if !strings.Contains(merged.Spec.OSImageURL, "sha256:") {
815				klog.Warningf("OSImageURL %q for MachineConfig %s is set using a tag instead of a digest. It is highly recommended to use a digest", merged.Spec.OSImageURL, merged.Name)
816			}
817	
818			// If the cluster admin overrides the OSImageURL field, it means they want
819			// to take over managing the OS image. If they then set osImageStream.name,
820			// this implies they want the MCO to manage the OS, which would override
821			// setting OSImageURL. A cluster admin should only be able to do one or the
822			// other; not both.
823			//
824			// In the future, we may want this to also consider some additional
825			// behaviors based on who set OSImageURL.
826			if pool.Spec.OSImageStream.Name != "" {
827				return nil, fmt.Errorf("cannot override MachineConfig osImageURL and set MachineConfigPool spec.osImageStream.name simultaneously")
828			}
829		}
830	
831		return merged, nil
832	}

834	// This function takes into account the current MachineConfig that is present
835	// on the MachineConfigPool. It first attempts to verify that the newly
836	// generated rendered MachineConfig is reconcilable against the current
837	// MachineConfig. If it is not reconcilable, the component MachineConfigs that
838	// were used to generate the rendered MachineConfig will be evaluated
839	// one-by-one to identify which MachineConfig is not reconcilable. The name of
840	// the unreconcilable MachineConfig is included in the returned error.
841	func generateAndValidateRenderedMachineConfig(
842		currentMC *mcfgv1.MachineConfig,
843		pool *mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool,
844		configs []*mcfgv1.MachineConfig,
845		cconfig *mcfgv1.ControllerConfig,
846		validationOverrides *opv1.IrreconcilableValidationOverrides,
847		osImageStreamSet *mcfgv1.OSImageStreamSet) (*mcfgv1.MachineConfig, error) {
848		source := getMachineConfigRefs(configs)
849		klog.V(4).Infof("Considering %d configs %s for MachineConfig generation", len(source), source)
850	
851		generated, err := generateRenderedMachineConfig(pool, configs, cconfig, osImageStreamSet)
852		if err != nil {
853			return nil, err
854		}
855	
856		klog.V(4).Infof("Considering generated MachineConfig %q", generated.Name)
857	
858		if fullErr := ctrlcommon.IsRenderedConfigReconcilable(currentMC, generated, validationOverrides); fullErr != nil {
859			fullMsg := fullErr.Error()
860	
861			// trying to match error reason suffix
862			for _, cfg := range configs {
863				singleErr := ctrlcommon.IsComponentConfigsReconcilable(
864					currentMC, []*mcfgv1.MachineConfig{cfg},
865					validationOverrides,
866				)
867				if singleErr == nil {
868					continue
869				}
870				singleMsg := singleErr.Error()
871	
872				// split off prefix
873				parts := strings.SplitN(singleMsg, ": ", 2)
874				if len(parts) == 2 {
875					reason := parts[1]
876					if strings.Contains(fullMsg, reason) {
877						klog.V(4).Infof("match found for base %q on reason %q", cfg.Name, reason)
878						return nil, fmt.Errorf("reconciliation failed between current config %q and base config %q: %s", currentMC.Name, cfg.Name, reason)
879					}
880				}
881			}
882	
883			// fallback
884			compErr := ctrlcommon.IsComponentConfigsReconcilable(currentMC, configs, validationOverrides)
885			return nil, fmt.Errorf("render reconciliation error: %v; component errors: %v", fullErr, compErr)
886		}
887	
888		klog.V(4).Infof("Rendered MachineConfig %q is reconcilable against %q", generated.Name, currentMC.Name)
889	
890		return generated, nil
891	}

893	// getOSImageStreamNameForPoolBootstrap implements the same inheritance logic as
894	// getOSImageStreamNameForPool but for the bootstrap scenario where we only have
895	// a slice of pools, not a lister.
896	func getOSImageStreamNameForPoolBootstrap(pool *mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool, pools []*mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool) string {
897		// If the pool explicitly sets an osImageStream, use it
898		if pool.Spec.OSImageStream.Name != "" {
899			return pool.Spec.OSImageStream.Name
900		}
901	
902		// Only custom pools inherit from worker
903		if !ctrlcommon.IsCustomPool(pool) {
904			return ""
905		}
906	
907		// Find worker pool in the provided slice
908		for _, p := range pools {
909			if p.Name == ctrlcommon.MachineConfigPoolWorker {
910				return p.Spec.OSImageStream.Name
911			}
912		}
913	
914		// Worker not found, use default
915		return ""
916	}

918	// RunBootstrap runs the render controller in bootstrap mode.
919	// For each pool, it matches the machineconfigs based on label selector and
920	// returns the generated machineconfigs and pool with CurrentMachineConfig status field set.
921	func RunBootstrap(pools []*mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool, configs []*mcfgv1.MachineConfig, cconfig *mcfgv1.ControllerConfig, osImageStream *mcfgv1.OSImageStream) ([]*mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool, []*mcfgv1.MachineConfig, error) {
922		var (
923			opools   []*mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool
924			oconfigs []*mcfgv1.MachineConfig
925		)
926		for _, pool := range pools {
927			pcs, err := getMachineConfigsForPool(pool, configs)
928			if err != nil {
929				return nil, nil, err
930			}
931			var osImageStreamSet *mcfgv1.OSImageStreamSet
932			if osImageStream != nil {
933				// Determine which stream name to use based on inheritance logic
934				streamName := getOSImageStreamNameForPoolBootstrap(pool, pools)
935				osImageStreamSet, err = osimagestream.GetOSImageStreamSetByName(osImageStream, streamName)
936				if err != nil {
937					return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("couldn't get the OSImageStream for pool %s %w", pool.Name, err)
938				}
939			}
940	
941			generated, err := generateRenderedMachineConfig(pool, pcs, cconfig, osImageStreamSet)
942			if err != nil {
943				return nil, nil, err
944			}
945	
946			source := getMachineConfigRefs(configs)
947	
948			pool.Spec.Configuration.Name = generated.Name
949			pool.Spec.Configuration.Source = source
950			pool.Status.Configuration.Name = generated.Name
951			pool.Status.Configuration.Source = source
952			opools = append(opools, pool)
953			oconfigs = append(oconfigs, generated)
954		}
955		return opools, oconfigs, nil
956	}

958	// getMachineConfigsForPool is called by RunBootstrap and returns configs that match label from configs for a pool.
959	func getMachineConfigsForPool(pool *mcfgv1.MachineConfigPool, configs []*mcfgv1.MachineConfig) ([]*mcfgv1.MachineConfig, error) {
960		selector, err := metav1.LabelSelectorAsSelector(pool.Spec.MachineConfigSelector)
961		if err != nil {
962			return nil, fmt.Errorf("invalid label selector: %w", err)
963		}
964	
965		var out []*mcfgv1.MachineConfig
966	
967		// If a pool with a nil or empty selector creeps in, it should match nothing
968		if selector.Empty() {
969			return out, nil
970		}
971		for idx, config := range configs {
972			if selector.Matches(labels.Set(config.Labels)) {
973				out = append(out, configs[idx])
974			}
975		}
976		if len(out) == 0 {
977			return nil, fmt.Errorf("couldn't find any MachineConfigs for pool: %v", pool.Name)
978		}
979		return out, nil
980	}

986			refs = append(refs, corev1.ObjectReference{Kind: machineconfigKind.Kind, Name: cfg.GetName(), APIVersion: machineconfigKind.GroupVersion().String()})

988	
989		return refs

135		ctrl.mcLister = mcInformer.Lister()

157	// Run executes the render controller.
158	func (ctrl *Controller) Run(workers int, stopCh <-chan struct{}) {
159		defer utilruntime.HandleCrash()
160		defer ctrl.queue.ShutDown()
161	
162		listerCaches := []cache.InformerSynced{ctrl.mcpListerSynced, ctrl.mcListerSynced, ctrl.ccListerSynced}
163	
164		// OSImageStreams and MCPs fetched only if FeatureGateOSStreams active
165		if ctrl.osImageStreamListerSynced != nil {
166			listerCaches = append(listerCaches, ctrl.osImageStreamListerSynced)
167		}
168		if !cache.WaitForCacheSync(stopCh, listerCaches...) {
169			return
170		}
171	
172		klog.Info("Starting MachineConfigController-RenderController")
173		defer klog.Info("Shutting down MachineConfigController-RenderController")
174	
175		for i := 0; i < workers; i++ {
176			go wait.Until(ctrl.worker, time.Second, stopCh)
177		}
178	
179		<-stopCh
180	}
